goal: Find one or more new orthogonal specialist signals or conditioning layers that work primarily where the base does not trade, use genuinely new information, pass window stability, and prove additive value through new entries upon integration.
global_constraints: Base active-signal-v1@1 is immutable — no re-tuning, no hour/day/threshold variations, no cosmetic changes.
Raw single-filter event entries (H1/H2 style) are permanently forbidden.
Every new candidate must first pass complement-first mode (base_absent surface) before any other evaluation.
Every prototype must contain at least 3 logical layers: new information layer + regime confirmation + density limiter.
Model-first approaches are forbidden until the low-friction wave (Wave A) completes.
No candidate may be a renamed variation of existing hour, dow, cl_*, rsi, iv_est static, or day-of-week patterns.
If permissive integration looks good but strict additive is weak — reject for hidden cannibalism.
Record both successes and reasons for rejection in research journal.

slices.title: Cannibalization, ownership and new-entry proof
slices.hypothesis: Formally prove that the new layer contributed genuinely new trades and new profit, not displaced base trades.
slices.objective: Formally prove that the new layer contributed genuinely new trades and new profit, not displaced base trades.
success_criteria: Direct proof exists showing which candidates genuinely expand the system.
evidence_requirements: Direct proof exists showing which candidates genuinely expand the system.

slices.title: Wave B — medium-friction branch
slices.hypothesis: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A was inconclusive.
slices.objective: Explore cross-symbol leadership, model-backed compression/routing, and event-state-model hybrids only if Wave A was inconclusive.
success_criteria: Wave B branches are evaluated for readiness; any that proceed produce at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.
evidence_requirements: Wave B branches are evaluated for readiness; any that proceed produce at least one candidate reaching standalone backtest.

slices.title: Walk-forward and final selection
slices.hypothesis: Run walk-forward validation on all surviving candidates and produce the final selection report with an honest answer on whether a new additive layer was found.
slices.objective: Run walk-forward validation on all surviving candidates and produce the final selection report with an honest answer on whether a new additive layer was found.
success_criteria: Walk-forward complete for all finalists and an honest final report exists stating whether a genuinely additive new layer was found.
evidence_requirements: Walk-forward complete for all finalists and an honest final report exists stating whether a genuinely additive new layer was found.
